panel_misses.detail: 'still asking: whom say ye that I am?' could read as demand-to-produce; independent flagged G4 CONDITIONAL (engine caught it, then panel-fixed via 'the Father put in Peter's mouth')

'Only His own people panicked' = law not grace; self-review passed the conviction
'the One who made it' imports creation; independent only CAUTIONed it (PASS), panel escalated to a fix

episode: 29 The Race He Could Never Win

panel_misses.detail: Series is Questions Jesus Asked + episode hinges on John 5:6 'Wilt thou be made whole?', but the draft PARAPHRASED the title question ('asks if he wants to be whole') and spent both Rule-8 quote slots on secondary verses (5:7 the excuse + 5:8 the command). All 4 panels caught it; self-review + independent both LOCKED. NOT a verbatim error (quotes were verbatim) — a quote-SELECTION error: the episode's signature verse was omitted from quotes. Fix: quote 5:6 + 5:8, excuse becomes narrator prose.

RECURRENCE of a 'fixed' class: 'He doesn't ask you to race. He asks if you still want it.' turns the verse-question onto the viewer as desire they must PRODUCE = the exact 'want it more' trap the episode kills. Self-review G4 (strengthened 2026-05-31) PASSED it; all 4 panels caught it. Fix: conviction reframed to grace exposing the false hope 'that you must close the distance to God before He acts' (grace acts first).
'Thirty-eight years isn't bad luck; it's a verdict' — 3 panels flagged as too-clever AND quasi-shaming (reads as a moral sentence on the man). Cut.

note: judged 4 panels (strong convergence on Rule-1 quote-selection + Rule-4/5 conviction). User chose a reshaped draft keeping the RACE spine (deliberately NOT folding in panel-4's strong 'he never said yes' insight, because that is the thread of the already-shipped #18 'He Never Said Yes', same John 5:6 — kept #29 distinct). 2-voice render (narrator + jesus on the two quotes), 59.04s, narrator atempo 1.1593, 158 words.

panel_misses.detail: Proof carried THREE spoken quotes (Isa 53:4 fragment + 53:5 full + 1 Peter 2:24 'bare our sins in his own body') vs the <=2 cap; all 3 external panels flagged it as the #1 issue; self-review's panel_request self-assessment did NOT flag it; DETERMINISTICALLY checkable (count double-quoted spans). Fix folded: dropped the Peter quote, kept both Isaiah halves.

53:4 quote 'we did esteem him stricken, smitten of God.' truncated mid-verse AND fabricated a terminal period (KJV reads '...smitten of God, and afflicted.'); the deterministic kjv_check MISSED it because only Isaiah 53:5 is cached in kjv_cache.json, so the 53:4 span fell through to 'ignored' (uncached-pericope coverage gap); a 'fixed' class recurred. Fix folded: quoted the full verse-ending clause '...smitten of God, and afflicted.'

note: judged 3 panels: consensus = change ONLY Proof (drop Peter -> 2 quotes, fix 53:4 verbatim). Kept Hook/Point/Conviction. User then reworked the Landing to identity-forward 'Look at the cross again. The punishment was real, but the guilt was never His. He took yours - into His own body.' (lands on what Christ did + ties back to the Point's 'punishing a man'). User added an ISAIAH VOICE for the two prophecy quotes (weighty voice UzI1NsMEV3ni5JRkRSls) -> 5-turn multi-voice, narrator atempo 1.2285, 59.02s. NOTE: _finalize.py does NOT clear _turns/*.mp3, so editing narration.md + re-finalizing silently reused stale per-turn audio -> had to delete _turns manually and run per_turn_synth directly.

episode: 31 The Light You Can Stand In

panel_misses.detail: spine claimed 'the light emptied the courtyard' but the accusers left in 8:9 by conscience, BEFORE the 8:12 light-claim; read the verse back into the scene; self-review + independent both PASSED it; external panel caught it
truncated John 8:12 before its own grace payoff 'but shall have the light of life'; the landing was hunting for grace that was sitting unused inside the verified verse
'go stand where she stood' = viewer-produced action; should be gift-framed ('where the guilty get to stand'); reviews passed it
spine rested on John 7:53-8:11 (bracketed/disputed in modern Bibles); acceptable for SLK devotional, would be a real risk for Awakeden apologetics; nothing flags it

note: first ep through strengthened gates; kjv_check false-positived on truncated quotes -> fixed truncation-aware. Switched spine to honest framing (conscience emptied court, not the light) + full verse + pillar-of-fire woven in.

episode: 32 The Door Was a Body (I AM the Door / invitation)
note: Series i-am. PART 1 of a two-dimension treatment of John 10:9. Iterated 3x to the user's direction (shepherd-as-door -> lead-with-I-AM/deity -> PERSONAL salvation INVITATION). Final = invitation-centered: deity ('I AM', too holy to speak) gives weight; heart = 'come in and be saved, the door is open for you AS YOU ARE before you fix a thing'; delivers the verse's saved/safe/fed/pasture payoff. ~151 words, 1 KJV quote (John 10:9 verbatim). 2-voice (narrator + jesus on the verse), 59.01s, narrator atempo 1.0435 (relaxed). panel_request.md on disk reflects the superseded v-b (deity) version, NOT this v-c invitation text -- it was user-directed + approved directly, not re-paneled. Cleared stale _turns + mp3 + meta before rendering (the _finalize stale-_turns trap). PART 2 (shepherd-as-the-gate dimension) is a separate sibling episode.

episode: 33 The Shepherd In The Gap (I AM the Door / shepherd-as-gate)

panel_misses.detail: Opens 'a sheepfold often had no gate -- the shepherd lay across it, his body the door' and asserts 'it was no figure of speech'. This rests on a CONTESTED preaching illustration; John 10:1-3 itself names a doorkeeper/porter + a door, so the immediate text has a gate. Agent flagged this to the user BEFORE rendering; user chose devotional latitude (SLK is devotional, not Awakeden apologetics) and shipped v-a as-is. ACCEPTED KNOWN RISK, not an undetected miss. Faithful core is sound (Jesus = only access; body-takes-the-wolf-first is grounded in John 10:11 'the good shepherd giveth his life for the sheep').

note: Series i-am. PART 2 of the two-dimension treatment of John 10:9 (sibling to #32 the invitation version). Same verse, complementary truth: #32 = open-door invitation, #33 = the door is a body in the gap where the wolf comes first (substitution/protection). Rendered direct (no external panel), v-a text essentially as written 2026-06-01. 2-voice (narrator + jesus on John 10:9 verbatim), 59.02s, narrator atempo 1.0337 (relaxed), ~150 words. NEW DIRECTION (user, 2026-06-02): deliberately explore MULTIPLE doctrinally-faithful dimensions per Bible topic -- one passage can speak several truths and serve more listeners. Non-negotiable: every dimension must be Bible-driven and fit evangelical biblical doctrine.

episode: 34 The Hunger Bread Can't Fill (I AM Bread / hunger-bread-cant-fill)

panel_misses.detail: draft ended conviction on 'the gap is shaped like Him' -- agent self-assessment PRE-FLAGGED it as the one near-conceit; all 4 panels agreed it reads writerly for a zero-Bible viewer. Fixed: 'asking created things to do what only Christ can do.'

draft landed on 'you never go hungry again' = my-relief + rule-6 over-promise (sounds like end of all earthly longing). Agent pre-flagged both; panels confirmed. Fixed: lands on the Bread Himself ('not a snack for the moment, but the life your soul was made for') + scope hedged to soul-hunger.

note: Series i-am, PART 1 of a 3-dimension Bread-of-Life cluster (John 6). 2-voice (narrator+jesus on 6:35), 58.97s, narrator atempo 0.88 (text short -> SLOWED, very spacious). Method: agent drafts -> ONE combined panel_request.md for all 3 dims -> user ran 4 external LLMs -> agent judged+synthesized. Notable: agent's pre-flight self-assessment predicted EVERY major panel hit across all 3 (strong calibration). Engine short_gate.py PASS (kjv/scope/relevance/freshness); manifest scope_rule 'spiritual-never-hunger' satisfied. NOTE: an autonomous engine run had produced a parallel 34/35/36 set (its #34 reintroduced the 'God-sized' cliche) then died; user chose the hand-panel-judged scripts, engine text cleared.

panel_misses.detail: draft truncated 6:51 BEFORE its gospel payoff 'and the bread that I will give is my flesh, which I will give for the life of the world' -- the cross, the only place it could live in the 3-dim set. Agent pre-flagged (same trap as #31); all 4 panels insisted on restoring it. Fixed: full verse quoted verbatim + narrator clarifier 'His own body, given' so flesh-clause reads as sacrifice not magic.

panel note: manna was a GOOD gift, don't frame receiving daily grace as the error; the trap is reducing Christ to crisis-management. Reframed: 'a top-up for today's crisis, then back to your own strength... He came to die your death.'

note: Series i-am, PART 2 of the Bread cluster. 2-voice (narrator+jesus on the FULL 6:51), 59.03s, narrator atempo 1.20 (full verse = 14.24s natural quote -> the tight one of the cluster, still under 1.30). The only draft in the set that carries the CROSS; panel consensus = non-negotiable. 'grave defeated'/'raise...at the last day' in-scope per 6:40,44. short_gate PASS.

panel_misses.detail: draft re-quoted 'him that cometh' + 'in no wise cast out' as fragmented echoes (rule-8 reads as narrator hitting quote-pace ~5x). Agent pre-flagged; panels confirmed. Fixed: dropped quote-marks on the echoes -> they flow as narrator prose (one spoken quote remains).

draft landing 'He will not turn you away' did NO new work (rule-7) -- just restated the verse. Fixed to panel-4's: 'He already read it, and still said come. The door you've been rehearsing at was never locked' (callbacks to the hook's 'record' + the point's 'door').
